channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: BC Conservative Party Leadership Debate Scheduled for May 9
item.description: Global BC will host a live BC Conservative Party leadership debate on Saturday, May 9, from 4:30 p.m. to 6 p.m., without commercial interruptions. The debate will be moderated by Ben O’Hara Byrne, Glo...

item.title: Instagram Removes Millions of Fake and Inactive Followers from Celebrity Accounts
item.description: Na so Instagram launch one major AI-driven clean-up wey dem dey call 'The Great Purge of 2026'. On May 6, 2026, millions of ghost accounts, bots, and fake followers just disappear from the platform wo...

item.title: Michelle Milthorpe Discusses Farrer Byelection and Independent Campaign
item.description: This Saturday sees a closely contested byelection in Farrer, the seat formerly held by Sussan Ley. The race is largely a two-horse contest between independent candidate Michelle Milthorpe and a repres...

item.title: Desi Lydic Critiques Trump’s Proposed One-Page Memo to End U.S.-Iran Conflict
item.description: Desi Lydic, host on 'The Daily Show,' expressed skepticism over reports that the United States and Iran are close to finalizing a one-page memo to end the ongoing conflict. The original report came fr...

item.title: 'Drug War: A Conspiracy of Silence' TV series to premiere internationally
item.description: ABS-CBN, one of the leading media and entertainment companies in the Philippines, is set to release its new TV series 'Drug War: A Conspiracy of Silence' to a global audience. The series aims to shed ...

item.title: Interim SAPS Chief Puleng Dimpane Faces Governance and Leadership Challenges
item.description: Following the suspension of SAPS National Commissioner General Fannie Masemola, President Cyril Ramaphosa appointed Lieutenant-General Puleng Dimpane as Acting National Commissioner. Dimpane, previous...

item.title: Alleged US Attack on Disabled Iranian Warship Raises Legal and Humanitarian Concerns
item.description: On March 4, 2026, the Iranian Navy ship IRIS Dena was reportedly attacked by the US submarine USS Charlotte off the coast of Sri Lanka. According to reports, the first torpedo disabled the ship, and a...

item.title: Quebec law offers limited answers as Indigenous families continue search for missing children
item.description: Five years after Quebec introduced Bill 79, a law allowing Indigenous families to access historical medical records of relatives who went missing or died in health-care institutions before 1992, many ...

item.title: The Closure of Australia’s Centre for Youth Literature and Its Impact on Teen Readers
item.description: In 2019, the State Library Victoria quietly retired the Centre for Youth Literature, a pioneering institution that had promoted teen reading and literature for decades. Originally founded in 1991 by A...

item.title: Federal Judge Sides with DOJ in Fulton County Election Records Case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) responded sharply to media criticism following a federal judge's ruling in a dispute over election materials in Fulton County, Georgia. In January, the FBI seized over ...

item.title: Armed cross-border attack reported on Naga villages in Manipur’s Kamjong district
item.description: Suspected Myanmar-based Kuki militant groups reportedly carried out a coordinated attack on Naga villages in Manipur’s Kamjong district, which lies along the India-Myanmar border. According to local N...

item.title: Saskatchewan authorities warn pet owners after cat contracts avian flu
item.description: A domestic cat in southeastern Saskatchewan died after contracting the H5N1 strain of avian influenza, according to a provincial news release. The cat appeared healthy on the morning of April 20 but d...

item.title: Court Petition Challenges Legality of Kenya’s New Health Financing System and SHA Operations
item.description: A constitutional petition has been filed at the High Court in Kiambu raising serious legal concerns about Kenya’s new healthcare financing framework, including the operations of the Social Health Auth...

item.title: U.S. Labour Market Remains Stable Despite Moderate Increase in Jobless Claims
item.description: The latest U.S. labour market data indicates stability despite a moderate increase in weekly jobless claims. According to the Labor Department, initial unemployment claims rose by 10,000 to a seasonal...

item.title: VP Shettima and APC Governors Submit Tinubu’s Nomination Forms Ahead of Direct Primaries
item.description: On Thursday, Vice President Kashim Shettima together with some APC governors and party leaders went to the APC National Working Committee (NWC) to submit President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s nomination and e...

item.title: Toronto's Shen Yun Performances Rescheduled for June with Increased Safety Measures
item.description: Toronto's scheduled Shen Yun performances at the Four Seasons Centre have been rescheduled to June, following concerns over safety and security. Organizers have confirmed that additional security prot...

item.title: Family of Errol Wendel clarifies body misidentification among Toboso 19 victims
item.description: The family of Errol Wendel, a community researcher, revealed that the body claimed to be his among the alleged victims of the Toboso encounter in Negros Occidental was actually misidentified. The auto...

item.title: Suvendu Adhikari Claims Aide Killed Following Bhabanipur Election Victory
item.description: Suvendu Adhikari, the prominent political figure from West Bengal, has claimed that his aide was tragically killed in the aftermath of his electoral victory over Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee in the ...

item.title: Zimbabwe Improves Relative Position While South Africa Slips in Global Misery Index
item.description: Zimbabwe has, for the first time, been ranked better than South Africa on economist Steve Hanke’s Annual Misery Index, a measure that typically combines factors such as inflation, unemployment, intere...

item.title: U.S.-Based Indigenous Groups Challenge B.C. Law Over Traditional Territories
item.description: British Columbia’s 2019 Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act (DRIPA), which aligns provincial law with the UN Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples, is facing new legal challe...
